title: Claude Code Users Report Rapid Rate Limit Drain, Suspect Bug [Update]
body: Claude Code users have been flooding GitHub and Reddit over the last few days with complaints that their usage limits are being exhausted at a suspiciously fast rate, with many reporting that sessions meant to last hours are literally burning out in minutes.

title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
body: Anthropic's new auto mode for Claude Code lets AI execute tasks with fewer approvals, reflecting a broader shift toward more autonomous tools that balance speed with safety through built-in safeguards.

title: Meet AutoDream : Claude Code's Clever New Trick for Memory Management
body: Claude Code's new AutoDream feature consolidates project memory, removes duplicates, and can be triggered manually with the /dream command.

title: Anthropic releases safer Claude Code 'auto mode' to avoid mass file deletions and other AI snafus
body: Anthropic has begun previewing "auto mode" inside of Claude Code. The company describes the new feature as a middle path between the app's default behavior, which sees Claude request approval for every file write and bash command,

title: Why Anthropic's Claude is the most powerful AI coding tool
body: It's not the quickest or the cheapest option, but Claude Code is so powerful that new releases rattle the stock markets.

title: Anthropic's Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
body: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

title: Anthropic just shipped an OpenClaw killer called Claude Code Channels, letting you message it over Telegram and Discord
